[30;1m182 | [0m[35m      if (/^;base64,/.test(inlineData)) {[0m
[30;1m183 | [0m[35m        // base64-encoded JSON string[0m
[30;1m184 | [0m[35m        log.debug('base64-encoded source map for', file.originalPath);[0m
[30;1m185 | [0m[35m        const buffer = Buffer.from(inlineData.slice(';base64,'.length), 'base64');[0m
[30;1m186 | [0m[35m        //@ts-ignore Assume the parsed charset is supported by Buffer.[0m
[30;1m187 | [0m[35m        sourceMapData(buffer.toString(charset));[0m
[30;1m188 | [0m[35m      } else if (inlineData.startsWith(',')) {[0m

   Rule Id: [34mBD000800[0m
       Tag: [34mSecurity.Backdoor.Obfuscation[0m
  Severity: [36mImportant[0m, Confidence: [36mHigh[0m
  Filename: [33m/karma-sourcemap-loader-0.tar/package/README.md[0m
   Pattern: [32mbase64|encodedcommand|obfuscate[0m
[30;1m18 | [0m[35mThis plug-in supports both inline and external source maps.[0m
[30;1m19 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m20 | [0m[35mInline source maps are located by searching "sourceMappingURL=" inside the javascript[0m
[30;1m21 | [0m[35mfile, both plain text and base64-encoded maps are supported.[0m
[30;1m22 | [0m[35m[0m
[30;1m23 | [0m[35mExternal source maps are located by appending ".map" to the javascript file name.[0m
[30;1m24 | [0m[35mSo if for example you have Hello.js, the preprocessor will try to load source map from[0m

5 matches found.
